claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my daughter Alex - she's 8 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I understand you have an 8-year-old daughter named Alex who lives with you at home.

To continue assessing your eligibility, could you please tell me: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! 

Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for her needs such as food, clothes, and her general upkeep?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for her - I provide everything she needs like food, clothes, school supplies, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or living in any residential accommodation provided by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not. She lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

One final question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for your daughter Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and provide for all her needs
- She lives with you at home full-time
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can proceed with making your application. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which means she is under 16 years old and meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, providing for her needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. Alex lives at home with the claimant full-time and is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child, and no one else is claiming for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one daughter, Alex, aged 8, who lives with them in the UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child, and no one else is claiming for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
